The correct answer is based on the understanding that C3 and C4 plant pathways help trace dietary shifts, with C4 grasses becoming more prominent in hominid diets over time. Research indicates that early hominids shifted from eating C3-based foods like nuts and fruits to incorporating more C4-based grasses or grazing animals. Thus, the transition was from nuts and fruits to species more focused on grasses.

\boxed{D}
